seoTitle: Mount Tully vs Mount Tyson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Tully and Mount Tyson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Tully vs Mount Tyson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Tully and Mount Tyson.
heading: Mount Tully vs Mount Tyson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Tully, Queensland and Mount Tyson, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Tyson has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Mount Tully (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Tully or Mount Tyson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Tully has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Mount Tyson scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Tully scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Tyson scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Tully or Mount Tyson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Tyson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
